ulf_hook: The earth is carved to hold the beasts. Three new lives stir in the mud. The silver calls from the frozen peaks.
grandpa_legend: We spent a good bit of time at West-Thwaite (Westshore) making sure the family would be fed, starting with those livestock pens. I felled a sturdy Oak (Iron-Bark) to get the fine wood we needed for that new X-frame chimney; it’s important to keep the smoke moving so an old soul can breathe. We even dug a deep moat down to the water level to keep the Wood-Wroth away from our new arrivals, and seeing those three piglets confirmed the Norns are smiling on our breeding efforts. It was a bitter lesson to find the structural damage left behind by the last raid—or the Norns' Trial, as I call it—but we've patched the walls. Now, we're preparing to smelt Silver (Frost-Tears), leaving the mud behind for the high peaks. Always check your supports after a fight; Wyrd's Weight can bring a roof down faster than a troll.
conrad_chronicle: The session focused on infrastructure development at the Westshore base, beginning with the construction of livestock pens and the relocation of boars. A large Oak tree was harvested for Fine Wood and Resin, which facilitated the addition of an X-frame chimney to the outbuilding for better ventilation. To secure the perimeter, a moat was excavated to the water table around the animal enclosure. Breeding was confirmed successful with the spawning of three piglets, providing a sustainable food source. After repairing structural damage caused by a previous raid event, the player announced the progression from the Iron Age to the Silver Age. Preparations are now underway to begin smelting Silver ore.
